annotation_status: draft — all golden scores require validation by two Avito domain experts (PM + Strategy) before production use

marketplace_relevance.description: How directly relevant is this to the classifieds/marketplace business model and portal product strategy?

event_scale.description: How large is the company, market, or user base affected? Global/top-3 platform = 9-10; small regional market = 4-5.

portability_to_avito.description: Can the product, business model, or regulatory pattern be applied to Avito Real Estate? Internal Avito data = 10.

urgency.description: How time-sensitive is the required response or awareness? Live product = 8-9; planning-horizon signal = 4-5.

novelty.description: How new is this pattern, model, or technology? First of its kind = 9-10; established pattern = 2-3.

priority_buckets.high: score 75-100 — include as main digest story
priority_buckets.medium: score 55-74 — include as main digest story (lower position)
priority_buckets.weak_signal: score 40-54 — include in weak signals section

calibration_cases.jtbd08-002_vs_jtbd08-003: Nearly identical scores (88 vs 87) — tests whether model correctly scores two equivalent events similarly
calibration_cases.jtbd08-005: High novelty + high portability but low scale — tests multi-dimensional reasoning
calibration_cases.jtbd08-013_and_jtbd08-015: portability_to_avito=10 internal cases — tests whether model handles 'this IS Avito' correctly
calibration_cases.jtbd08-016: portability_to_avito=9 despite Yandex being a competitor, not Avito — tests whether model understands portability as 'applicability of pattern' not 'this IS Avito'

notes: All golden scores were derived from the weekly digest priority labels (high/medium) and the scoring rubric in agent-spec.md.
Avito-internal articles (jtbd08-013, jtbd08-015) receive portability_to_avito=10 by definition — this is intentional and correct.
Score variance of ±5 between annotators is acceptable on individual cases. Variance > 10 indicates a calibration issue.
The scoring formula produces 0-100 range: (sum of weighted 0-10 scores) × 10.
